title: Implement trace context propagation and conversation span management
purpose: Establish the tracing foundation for liminal: a TraceContext type that carries trace_id and span_id through conversations and messages, and automatic span creation for conversations. Conversations auto-create spans at start, messages inherit their conversation's trace context, and child conversations reference their parent's span as a parent. Trace context propagation is transparent to application code — no manual instrumentation required.
task: Create the tracing module at crates/liminal/src/tracing/ with mod.rs, context.rs, and span.rs. Define TraceContext as a lightweight carrier of trace_id (128-bit) and span_id (64-bit) following W3C Trace Context semantics. Implement SpanGuard for RAII-style span lifecycle. Wire conversation start to create a span and attach the trace context to all messages within that conversation. Child conversations link to their parent's span.

requirements.title: Create tracing module skeleton
requirements.spec: crates/liminal/src/tracing/mod.rs SHALL declare pub mod for context and span. mod.rs SHALL contain only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports. mod.rs SHALL NOT contain any function, struct, trait, or impl definitions.
acceptance: crates/liminal/src/tracing/mod.rs exists
mod.rs declares pub mod context and pub mod span
mod.rs contains zero function definitions, zero struct definitions, zero trait definitions
cargo check -p liminal passes

files: crates/liminal/src/tracing/mod.rs:1 — currently only `// tracing module`; replace with declarations/re-exports only.
crates/liminal/src/metrics/mod.rs:1-7 — model for a module root containing only `pub mod` plus `pub use`.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/mod.rs:1-15 — another module-root convention example with declarations first, blank line, re-exports.
docs/design/observability/design.json:108-120 — design inventory names `tracing/mod.rs`, `context.rs`, and `span.rs` and says module root is pub-mod/pub-use only.
context: `mod.rs` files in this crate are intentionally thin; `metrics/mod.rs` and `protocol/mod.rs` contain no functions, structs, traits, or impls.
`crates/liminal/src/tracing/mod.rs` already exists, so this is a replacement of a placeholder rather than a true create.
CN3 is stricter than normal Rust style: no helper constants, tests, functions, or private imports in `mod.rs`; only `pub mod context;`, `pub mod span;`, and `pub use ...` lines.
Re-exporting `TraceContext`, `Span`, `FinishedSpan`, collector APIs from this module will make `lib.rs` re-exports cleaner in R2.
scout.approach: Replace the placeholder `tracing/mod.rs` with `pub mod context;` and `pub mod span;`, followed by `pub use context::TraceContext;` and the span re-exports the later requirements need, such as `FinishedSpan`, `NoopCollector`, `Span`, `SpanCollector`, and install/accessor functions if implemented. Keep the file formatted like `metrics/mod.rs`: declarations first, blank line, re-exports only. Do not put tests or helper code here; unit tests should live in `context.rs`/`span.rs` or integration tests.
scout.notes: Because `context.rs` and `span.rs` do not exist yet, adding module declarations before creating those files will temporarily break `cargo check`; implement R1 together with R3/R6 file creation or create empty compiling files during the same change.

files_changed.note: Replaced placeholder with pub mod declarations for context/span and pub use re-exports only.

dev.how: The tracing module root now declares `pub mod context;` and `pub mod span;`, then re-exports the public tracing APIs. It contains no functions, structs, traits, impls, imports, tests, or helper logic, satisfying the thin-module convention.

checklist.note: TraceContext is re-exported from the tracing module skeleton.

No existing code imports `liminal::tracing`, so adding the module is low-risk once the child files compile.
Keep ordering predictable: add `pub mod tracing;` near the other module declarations and `pub use tracing::TraceContext;` with the other root re-exports.

Do not expose span collector internals from `lib.rs` unless hidden tests or API requirements demand it; the acceptance only requires `TraceContext` at the crate root.
scout.notes: If `TraceContext` is not re-exported from `tracing/mod.rs`, `pub use tracing::TraceContext;` will fail; either re-export it in `mod.rs` or use `pub use tracing::context::TraceContext;`, with the former matching existing subsystem style.

requirements.title: Define TraceContext with trace_id and span_id
requirements.spec: TraceContext SHALL carry a trace_id (u128) and a span_id (u64) following W3C Trace Context semantics. TraceContext SHALL implement Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq. TraceContext SHALL provide new_root() that generates a fresh trace_id and span_id, and child() that creates a new span_id under the same trace_id. TraceContext SHALL NOT use any external tracing crate — IDs are self-generated.
acceptance: TraceContext has fields trace_id: u128 and span_id: u64
TraceContext derives Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq
TraceContext::new_root() returns a TraceContext with non-zero trace_id and span_id
ctx.child() returns a TraceContext with the same trace_id but a different span_id
Two calls to new_root() produce different trace_ids

files: crates/liminal/src/tracing/context.rs:1-new — create target for `TraceContext`; file is currently absent.
crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:4-34 — style example for public types: derives, public fields, constructor, `#[must_use]`.
crates/liminal/Cargo.toml:11-14 — current liminal dependencies contain `beamr` and `thiserror`, no direct tracing or random dependency.
Cargo.toml:14-23 — workspace lints deny unsafe code, unwrap/expect, and panics.
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-006.json:49-65 — R3 requirement and acceptance text for fields, derives, root/child behavior.
context: Use a lightweight `#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)] pub struct TraceContext { pub trace_id: u128, pub span_id: u64 }` unless you deliberately add accessors; public fields match the acceptance wording exactly.
Existing public types derive `Debug`; this is mandatory under CN4 and workspace clippy is pedantic.
`TraceContext::new_root()` must generate both IDs, while `child()` preserves `trace_id` and only generates a new non-zero `span_id`.
There is no external tracing/opentelemetry dependency in the crate and the brief explicitly forbids adding one.
scout.approach: Create `context.rs` with the `TraceContext` struct, `new_root()`, `child()`, and simple `trace_id()`/`span_id()` accessors that R4 will use for round-trip tests. Implement `new_root()` in terms of private ID-generation helpers so R4 can refine the source without changing the public API. Add unit tests in this file for non-zero IDs, new-root trace-id uniqueness, child trace-id preservation, and child span-id difference.
scout.notes: The requirement says W3C semantics; W3C trace IDs and span IDs must not be all zero. Make the non-zero guarantee part of the generator helper rather than trying to repair IDs separately in every public constructor.

files_changed.note: Defined TraceContext with trace_id/span_id fields, root/child generation, accessors, and tests.

dev.how: `TraceContext` is a lightweight `Copy` carrier with public `trace_id: u128` and `span_id: u64` fields and derives `Clone`, `Copy`, `Debug`, `PartialEq`, and `Eq`. `new_root()` generates non-zero trace/span IDs, while `child()` preserves the trace ID and generates a different non-zero span ID.

checklist.note: TraceContext carries the required trace ID and span ID.

stories.note: The context carrier and child-context API provide the correlation primitive used by automatic conversation propagation.

requirements.title: Implement TraceContext ID generation
requirements.spec: TraceContext SHALL generate trace_id and span_id using a deterministic-safe source. In production, IDs SHALL be generated from a thread-local random source. TraceContext SHALL provide from_ids(trace_id, span_id) for reconstructing a context from serialized data. SHALL NOT use std::time or wall clock for ID generation.
acceptance: TraceContext::from_ids(trace_id, span_id) reconstructs the context
from_ids round-trips correctly: from_ids(ctx.trace_id(), ctx.span_id()) == ctx
No std::time or SystemTime usage in ID generation
IDs are non-zero in all generated contexts

files: crates/liminal/src/tracing/context.rs:1-new — extend the new `TraceContext` implementation with ID source and `from_ids`.
crates/liminal/Cargo.toml:11-14 — no direct random dependency exists today; strict thread-local random generation may require adding one despite the brief’s file list.
Cargo.lock:738-746 — `rand 0.10.1` is already present transitively, but liminal cannot use it without a direct dependency.
Cargo.toml:14-23 — lints deny `unsafe`, `unwrap`, `expect`, and `panic`, affecting random-byte fallback/error handling.
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-006.json:68-83 — R4 acceptance, including no `std::time`/`SystemTime` for ID generation.
context: Rust std has no general random API; a true production thread-local random source will need a direct dependency such as `rand`/`getrandom`, even though the generated brief’s file list omits `Cargo.toml`.
Do not use wall-clock time, `Instant`, counters alone, process IDs, or hashes of time for IDs; R6 may use `Instant` for span duration, but R4 may not use time for ID generation.

`from_ids(trace_id, span_id)` is for reconstructing serialized data; the acceptance says it reconstructs, not rejects, so avoid returning `Option` here unless tests specifically require zero rejection.

Generated contexts must be non-zero; parsed/reconstructed contexts can be handled consistently with W3C by making `from_traceparent` reject zero IDs in R5 while `from_ids` remains a raw constructor if needed.
scout.approach: Add private `next_trace_id()`/`next_span_id()` helpers backed by a thread-local RNG and loop until the generated value is non-zero. Then implement `pub const fn from_ids(trace_id: u128, span_id: u64) -> Self`, plus `pub const fn trace_id(&self) -> u128` and `pub const fn span_id(&self) -> u64` for tests and callers. If adding `rand`, add it as a direct liminal dependency and use a thread-local generator; if the project owner refuses manifest changes, document that the strict production-random acceptance cannot be met with std-only code.
scout.notes: The direct-dependency gotcha is the big time sink: transitive `rand` in `Cargo.lock` is not usable from `liminal`. Also avoid `unwrap()` on random APIs; propagate or retry safely, because workspace lints deny unwrap/expect/panic even outside production paths.

files_changed.note: Added thread-local random ID generation, `from_ids`, and ID accessors.
Added direct `rand` dependency for production thread-local random generation.
Updated liminal package dependency list to include rand.

dev.how: ID generation uses `rand::rng()` as the production thread-local random source and retries until generated IDs are non-zero. `from_ids`, `trace_id()`, and `span_id()` provide reconstruction and round-tripping without using `std::time`, `SystemTime`, wall-clock values, counters, or external tracing crates.
dev.deviation: The brief file list did not include manifest changes, but the scout notes identified that strict thread-local random generation cannot be met with std-only Rust and requires a direct random dependency. I added `rand = "0.10.1"` to `crates/liminal/Cargo.toml`; this follows the scouted approach's conditional dependency guidance.

checklist.note: TraceContext reconstructs and round-trips from raw IDs, and generated contexts are non-zero.

stories.note: Self-generated context IDs support transparent correlation without application-provided IDs.

acceptance.criterion: IDs are non-zero in all generated contexts
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/context.rs:74-90 loops until trace/span candidates are non-zero; tests `new_root_generates_non_zero_ids` and `child_preserves_trace_id_and_changes_span_id` passed.

requirements.title: Implement TraceContext serialization to W3C traceparent format
requirements.spec: TraceContext SHALL provide to_traceparent() -> String that renders the context as a W3C traceparent header value (version-trace_id-parent_id-flags format: 00-{32 hex}-{16 hex}-01). TraceContext SHALL provide from_traceparent(s: &str) -> Option<TraceContext> for parsing. SHALL NOT depend on any external serialization crate for this.
acceptance: to_traceparent() produces a string matching 00-{32 hex chars}-{16 hex chars}-01
from_traceparent(ctx.to_traceparent()) == Some(ctx)
from_traceparent with invalid input returns None
from_traceparent with wrong version prefix returns None

files: crates/liminal/src/tracing/context.rs:1-new — add `to_traceparent` and `from_traceparent` here without serialization crates.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:209-238 — current conversation wire frames have no trace context fields; do not change these for R5.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec.rs:134-180 — current encoded payload lengths assume no traceparent field; changing wire encoding would ripple into codec tests.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec/known.rs:139-176 — conversation decoder currently reads only conversation id plus subject/payload/close/error fields.
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-006.json:86-101 — R5 acceptance for exact `00-{32 hex}-{16 hex}-01` format and invalid parsing.
context: This requirement is only string serialization/parsing on `TraceContext`; the boundary says not to implement protocol wire propagation or external serializers here.
Use fixed-width lowercase hex: `format!("00-{trace_id:032x}-{span_id:016x}-01")` is the straightforward shape, but parsing should not rely on panicking slices.
`from_traceparent` should validate four hyphen-separated parts, version `00`, trace-id length 32, span-id length 16, flags `01`, all hex, and reject all-zero IDs.
Invalid input and wrong version must return `None`, not an error or panic.
scout.approach: Implement `to_traceparent()` with fixed-width hex formatting and `from_traceparent(s: &str) -> Option<TraceContext>` with explicit split/length/version/flags checks before calling `u128::from_str_radix` and `u64::from_str_radix`. Keep this dependency-free; no `serde`, no `tracing`, no `opentelemetry`. Add unit tests for round-trip, wrong version, wrong flags, missing sections, bad lengths, non-hex input, and zero trace/span IDs if following W3C validity strictly.
scout.notes: Do not add `traceparent` fields to `Frame::Conversation*` in this brief. The design says wire encoding is handled by the protocol cluster, and touching `frame.rs`/`codec.rs` would push files near the 500-line limit and break many codec expectations.

files_changed.note: Added W3C traceparent rendering/parsing and invalid-input tests.

dev.how: `to_traceparent()` renders the fixed `00-{32 hex trace_id}-{16 hex span_id}-01` format with lowercase, zero-padded hex. `from_traceparent()` validates part count, version, flags, exact lengths, hex content, and non-zero IDs, returning `None` for invalid input without any serialization dependency.

checklist.note: TraceContext serializes to and parses from traceparent strings with correct round-tripping.

stories.note: Traceparent support lets contexts be reconstructed across serialized boundaries for later propagation work.

acceptance.criterion: to_traceparent() produces a string matching 00-{32 hex chars}-{16 hex chars}-01
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/context.rs:41-44 formats `00-{:032x}-{:016x}-01`; test `tracing::context::tests::traceparent_round_trips` asserts length 55 and passed.

acceptance.criterion: from_traceparent(ctx.to_traceparent()) == Some(ctx)
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/context.rs:46-71 parses traceparent; test `tracing::context::tests::traceparent_round_trips` passed.

acceptance.criterion: from_traceparent with invalid input returns None
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/context.rs:54-68 rejects malformed, non-hex, and zero IDs; test `tracing::context::tests::invalid_traceparents_return_none` passed.

acceptance.criterion: from_traceparent with wrong version prefix returns None
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/context.rs:54-56 requires version `00`; test `tracing::context::tests::invalid_traceparents_return_none` includes wrong version and passed.

requirements.title: Define Span type for conversation lifecycle
requirements.spec: Span SHALL represent an active trace span with a name, a TraceContext, an optional parent TraceContext, and a start timestamp. Span SHALL implement Debug. Span SHALL provide finish() that records the span duration. Span SHALL NOT send data anywhere — it is a local record. Exporters (future work) read finished spans.
acceptance: Span has fields: name (String), context (TraceContext), parent (Option<TraceContext>), start (Instant)
Span implements Debug
Span::new(name, context, parent) creates a span with Instant::now() as start
span.finish() returns a FinishedSpan with the elapsed duration

files: crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:1-new — create target for `Span` and `FinishedSpan`; file is currently absent.
crates/liminal/src/tracing/context.rs:1-new — `Span` depends on `TraceContext` from R3/R4.
crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:36-72 — style example for simple public handle methods, `Debug`, `#[must_use]`, and documented errors.
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-006.json:104-119 — R6 acceptance for fields, `Instant::now()`, and `finish()`.
docs/design/observability/design.json:138-170 — constraints: file length, no panics, public Debug, no manual context passing.
context: `Instant` is allowed for span duration measurement; the no-wall-clock constraint applies to ID generation, not `Span::start`.
`Span` should derive or implement `Debug`; all public types need `Debug`.
`Span::new(name, context, parent)` should accept `impl Into<String>` for ergonomics but store an owned `String`, matching the spec field.
`finish()` should consume `self` so a span cannot be finished twice accidentally and so `FinishedSpan` is constructed from the owned fields without clones.
scout.approach: Create `span.rs` with `use std::time::{Duration, Instant};` and `use crate::tracing::TraceContext;`. Define `#[derive(Debug)] pub struct Span { name: String, context: TraceContext, parent: Option<TraceContext>, start: Instant }` plus `pub fn new(name: impl Into<String>, context: TraceContext, parent: Option<TraceContext>) -> Self` using `Instant::now()`. Implement `pub fn finish(self) -> FinishedSpan` by computing `self.start.elapsed()` and moving name/context/parent into the finished record.
scout.notes: If tests need to inspect `start`, private fields will be awkward; however R7’s “FinishedSpan constructed only from Span::finish()” points toward private fields plus accessors. For `Span`, consider accessors for `name`, `context`, `parent`, and `start` rather than public mutable fields.

files_changed.note: Defined active Span with name/context/parent/start, constructor, accessors, and finish.

dev.how: `Span` stores an owned name, `TraceContext`, optional parent `TraceContext`, and `Instant` start timestamp. `Span::new` captures `Instant::now()`, and `finish()` consumes the active span, computes elapsed duration, and returns a `FinishedSpan`. The span itself emits nothing externally except through the optional collector path required by R9.

checklist.note: Conversation lifecycle spans can carry their context, parent, and measured duration.

stories.note: Span construction binds trace context to conversation lifecycle records.

acceptance.criterion: Span has fields: name (String), context (TraceContext), parent (Option<TraceContext>), start (Instant)
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:21-27 defines `Span` with `name: String`, `context: TraceContext`, `parent: Option<TraceContext>`, and `start: Instant`.

acceptance.criterion: Span implements Debug
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:21 derives `Debug` for `Span`.

acceptance.criterion: Span::new(name, context, parent) creates a span with Instant::now() as start
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:29-42 defines `Span::new` and sets `start: Instant::now()`; test `tracing::span::tests::span_start_is_now` passed.

requirements.title: Implement FinishedSpan as an immutable record
requirements.spec: FinishedSpan SHALL be an immutable record containing the span name, TraceContext, optional parent TraceContext, and duration. FinishedSpan SHALL implement Debug and Clone. FinishedSpan is the unit that span exporters (future work) will consume.
acceptance: FinishedSpan has fields: name, context, parent, duration
FinishedSpan implements Debug and Clone
FinishedSpan is constructed only from Span::finish()
FinishedSpan duration matches the elapsed time between Span creation and finish

files: crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:1-new — implement `FinishedSpan` in the same new file as `Span`.
crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:73-107 — existing immutable snapshot-style public records and accessors to mirror.
crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:223-230 — snapshot construction pattern: build owned records from live state.
crates/liminal/tests/metrics_registry.rs:11-16 — integration-test style for asserting public Debug/static properties.
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-006.json:121-137 — R7 acceptance, especially construction only through `Span::finish()`.
context: `FinishedSpan` must derive `Debug` and `Clone`; deriving `PartialEq/Eq` is optional but useful except `Duration` supports equality, so it is safe if desired.

To make “constructed only from `Span::finish()`” meaningful, keep `FinishedSpan` fields private and expose read-only accessors; public fields would let downstream code construct or mutate it directly.
The existing metrics snapshots are owned records, not borrowed views; follow that pattern by moving/cloning owned `String`, `TraceContext`, `Option<TraceContext>`, and `Duration`.
Duration tests should compare lower/upper bounds rather than exact equality to avoid flakiness.
scout.approach: Define `#[derive(Debug, Clone)] pub struct FinishedSpan { name: String, context: TraceContext, parent: Option<TraceContext>, duration: Duration }` with `#[must_use]` getters like `name(&self) -> &str`, `context(&self) -> TraceContext`, `parent(&self) -> Option<TraceContext>`, and `duration(&self) -> Duration`. Keep the constructor private, e.g. `fn new(...) -> Self`, and call it only from `Span::finish()`. Add a test that creates a span, sleeps or spins minimally if needed, finishes it, and asserts the fields match and duration is non-zero or at least not greater than a measured outer elapsed bound.
scout.notes: If hidden tests expect public fields because the acceptance says “has fields,” private fields may require those tests to use accessors. A compromise is `pub(crate) fn new` plus public getters; avoid public struct fields if the “only from Span::finish()” criterion is enforced.

files_changed.note: Added immutable FinishedSpan record with Clone/Debug and read-only accessors.

dev.how: `FinishedSpan` contains the finished span name, context, parent, and duration, derives `Debug` and `Clone`, and exposes only read-only accessors. Its constructor is private and is only invoked by `Span::finish()`, preserving the immutable exporter-record contract.

checklist.note: Finished spans are owned immutable records produced from active span completion.

stories.note: Finished spans preserve the trace context needed to correlate completed conversation work.

acceptance.criterion: FinishedSpan has fields: name, context, parent, duration
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:77-83 defines `FinishedSpan` with `name`, `context`, `parent`, and `duration` fields.

acceptance.criterion: FinishedSpan implements Debug and Clone
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:77 derives `Debug, Clone`; test `tracing::span::tests::finished_span_is_clone_debug` passed.

acceptance.criterion: FinishedSpan is constructed only from Span::finish()
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:85-98 has private `FinishedSpan::new`; production call site is `Span::finish` at crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:64-74.

acceptance.criterion: FinishedSpan duration matches the elapsed time between Span creation and finish
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:64-68 uses `self.start.elapsed()`; test `tracing::span::tests::span_finish_returns_finished_span` bounded duration against an outer elapsed timer and passed.

requirements.title: Conversation span creation with automatic context propagation
requirements.spec: When a conversation starts, a Span SHALL be created with the conversation ID as the span name. The span's TraceContext SHALL become the conversation's trace context. All messages within the conversation SHALL inherit this TraceContext. When a child conversation is spawned, its span SHALL reference the parent conversation's TraceContext as the parent span. This propagation SHALL be automatic — application code SHALL NOT need to pass trace contexts manually.
acceptance: Starting a conversation creates a Span with a fresh TraceContext
The conversation's messages carry the conversation's TraceContext
A child conversation's Span has parent set to the parent conversation's TraceContext
No manual TraceContext passing is required by application code

files: crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:1-new — brief says this is the only modified file for conversation span helpers.
crates/liminal/src/conversation/mod.rs:1 — conversation module root is empty, so there is no existing public conversation API to hook.
crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor.rs:1 — actor implementation is empty; no lifecycle start/finish code currently exists.
crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:1 — no conversation/message types currently store context.
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-007.json:67-75 — future logging expects a thread-local active `TraceContext` set on span entry/cleared on exit.
context: The acceptance talks about “starting a conversation,” “messages,” and “child conversation,” but the current conversation module is blank; satisfying this inside only `span.rs` means providing tracing primitives/facades that future conversation code will use.
The generated brief task mentions a `SpanGuard` for RAII-style lifecycle even though the individual R8 acceptance does not; adding a small guard now will help OBS-007 thread-local logging.
Automatic propagation means application-facing APIs should not accept `TraceContext` parameters; the conversation/span abstraction should derive and expose message context internally.
Do not implement structured logging here; only set up span/context mechanics so OBS-007 can read active context later.
scout.approach: In `span.rs`, add a small conversation-oriented helper such as `SpanGuard` or `ConversationSpan` that owns a `Span`, exposes `context() -> TraceContext`, can create a child with `child(conversation_id)` by calling `self.context.child()` and setting `parent: Some(self.context)`, and can provide the context that message construction should attach. If you implement thread-local current context now, keep it minimal and tracing-only: set on guard creation/enter and restore/clear on drop, with accessors for future logging, but do not emit logs. Because no conversation actor exists, hidden tests will likely exercise these helpers directly; if they instead expect real conversation types, you will need to expand `conversation/types.rs` despite the brief’s file list.
scout.notes: This is the requirement most likely to suffer scope mismatch. Current code has no message envelope or conversation lifecycle to modify, while protocol conversation frames also lack trace fields; avoid large protocol/conversation rewrites unless tests force it, and document the helper as the integration point for future conversation actor work.

files_changed.note: Added ConversationSpan and SpanGuard helpers for automatic conversation context creation, child linkage, message context access, and thread-local current context.

dev.how: Because the conversation actor/types are currently empty, propagation is implemented as the tracing primitive/facade the future actor will use: `ConversationSpan::new/root` starts a conversation with a fresh root `TraceContext`, `message_context()` returns the conversation context for message attachment, and `child()`/`with_parent()` create child conversation spans whose parent is the parent conversation context. `SpanGuard` provides RAII-style automatic current-context installation/restoration without application-facing TraceContext parameters.

checklist.note: Conversation span helpers create fresh contexts for roots, inherit context for messages, and link child spans to parent contexts.

stories.note: Application code can start conversation spans by conversation ID and use guard/current-context propagation without manually passing trace contexts through messages.

acceptance.criterion: Starting a conversation creates a Span with a fresh TraceContext
acceptance.evidence: Fixed in crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:8-14: `Conversation::start` creates `ConversationSpan::root`; `ConversationSpan::root` creates `Span::new(..., TraceContext::new_root(), None)` at crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:132-137. Test `conversation::types::tests::starting_conversation_creates_named_span_with_fresh_trace_context` passed.

acceptance.criterion: The conversation's messages carry the conversation's TraceContext
acceptance.evidence: Fixed in crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:23-26: `Conversation::message` creates `ConversationMessage` with `self.span.message_context()`; message stores `trace_context` at lines 49-52. Test `conversation::types::tests::messages_inherit_conversation_trace_context_automatically` passed.

acceptance.criterion: A child conversation's Span has parent set to the parent conversation's TraceContext
acceptance.evidence: Fixed in crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:16-21 via `self.span.child`; underlying `ConversationSpan::child/with_parent` sets `Some(parent)` at crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:139-149. Test `conversation::types::tests::child_conversation_references_parent_trace_context` passed.

acceptance.criterion: No manual TraceContext passing is required by application code
acceptance.evidence: Fixed public API uses `Conversation::start`, `Conversation::message(payload)`, and `Conversation::spawn_child` in crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:8-26 with no TraceContext parameter. Test `conversation::types::tests::messages_inherit_conversation_trace_context_automatically` exercises message creation without passing TraceContext.

issues: Initial actual code only added tracing helpers while crates/liminal/src/conversation/mod.rs, actor.rs, types.rs, and patterns.rs were empty, so conversation start/message propagation was not wired into an actual conversation API.
The first hardening version tripped clippy `missing_const_for_fn` for `Conversation::message`; fixed before final verification.
fixes: Added crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs with `Conversation` and `ConversationMessage` so conversations create spans, messages automatically inherit trace context, and child conversations link to the parent context.
Updated crates/liminal/src/conversation/mod.rs to re-export the new conversation primitives while remaining a thin module.
Made `Conversation::message` a `const fn` to satisfy strict clippy after hardening.

requirements.title: SpanCollector trait for finished span export
requirements.spec: SpanCollector SHALL be a trait with a single method: on_span(span: FinishedSpan). This is the extension point for span exporters (e.g., Jaeger, OTLP — future work). A NoopCollector SHALL be provided that discards all spans. The active collector SHALL be set globally via an atomic pointer, matching the MetricsRegistry zero-overhead pattern. When no collector is installed, span finishing SHALL be a no-op.
acceptance: SpanCollector trait has fn on_span(&self, span: FinishedSpan)
NoopCollector implements SpanCollector and discards spans
A global optional SpanCollector is set via atomic pointer
When no collector is installed, Span::finish() performs no work beyond computing duration

files: crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:1-new — implement `SpanCollector`, `NoopCollector`, global install/check, and collector call from `Span::finish()`.
crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:15-17 — existing global zero-overhead pattern uses an atomic enabled flag plus `OnceLock`.
crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:265-290 — existing install/accessor API and already-installed error behavior for globals.
crates/liminal/src/metrics/mod.rs:3-7 — re-export pattern for global install/accessor functions from subsystem module.
Cargo.toml:14-23 — `unsafe_code = deny` is critical when interpreting the brief’s “atomic pointer” requirement.
context: The brief says “atomic pointer,” but existing `MetricsRegistry` uses `AtomicBool` plus `OnceLock`, not `AtomicPtr`; dereferencing a raw atomic pointer to a trait object would normally require unsafe, which workspace lints forbid.
Define `SpanCollector: std::fmt::Debug + Send + Sync + 'static` with `fn on_span(&self, span: FinishedSpan)`; `NoopCollector` should derive `Debug`, `Clone`, `Copy`, `Default`.
`Span::finish()` must always return `FinishedSpan`; if no collector is installed, it should only compute duration/build the record and skip collector work via a cheap atomic check.
If a collector is installed and `finish()` must also return the finished record, call `collector.on_span(finished.clone())` because the trait takes ownership.
scout.approach: Mirror the metrics global API as closely as the lints allow: define a global installed flag (or a safe atomic marker) plus `OnceLock<Box<dyn SpanCollector>>`/`OnceLock<&'static dyn SpanCollector>` and functions such as `install_span_collector`, `span_collector_enabled`, and private/public `global_span_collector`. If you add an install error for duplicate installs, make it `Debug` and implement `Display` + `std::error::Error`, following `MetricRegistrationError`. If hidden acceptance literally scans for `AtomicPtr`, use it only as a non-dereferenced installed marker alongside `OnceLock`; do not introduce unsafe raw-pointer dereferencing in production code.
scout.notes: A no-collector fast path should be checked before any clone or dynamic dispatch. Also consider test isolation: global one-shot collectors are hard to reset between tests, so unit tests for collector behavior may need to install once in a dedicated test binary or use a single integration test that tolerates global state.

files_changed.note: Added SpanCollector trait, NoopCollector, global install/accessor functions, and no-collector fast path.
Re-exported collector APIs from the tracing module root.

dev.how: `SpanCollector` is a `Debug + Send + Sync + 'static` trait with `fn on_span(&self, span: FinishedSpan)`, and `NoopCollector` discards spans. The global collector uses a safe `OnceLock<Box<dyn SpanCollector>>` plus an `AtomicPtr<()>` installed marker for the zero-overhead enabled check without unsafe dereferencing. When no collector is installed, `Span::finish()` only computes duration and builds/returns the finished span; when installed, it clones the finished record for the collector.

checklist.note: Finished spans can be optionally handed to a globally installed collector, and no collector means no dynamic-dispatch/export work.

acceptance.criterion: SpanCollector trait has fn on_span(&self, span: FinishedSpan)
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:270-272 defines `pub trait SpanCollector` with `fn on_span(&self, span: FinishedSpan)`.

acceptance.criterion: NoopCollector implements SpanCollector and discards spans
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:274-281 defines `NoopCollector` and its `on_span` drops the span; test `tracing::span::tests::noop_collector_discards_spans` passed.

acceptance.criterion: A global optional SpanCollector is set via atomic pointer
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:13-15 defines `SPAN_COLLECTOR_MARKER: AtomicPtr<()>` plus `GLOBAL_SPAN_COLLECTOR`; install stores the marker at lines 315-328 and access gates via atomic load at lines 330-342, matching the existing non-blocking global-enabled pattern.

acceptance.criterion: When no collector is installed, Span::finish() performs no work beyond computing duration
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:64-74 computes duration and only calls `collector.on_span` when `global_span_collector()` returns Some; with default null marker, `global_span_collector` returns None at lines 330-342.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement structured logging — that is OBS-007.
SHALL NOT implement any metrics collection — that is OBS-001 through OBS-005.
SHALL NOT implement span export to Jaeger, OTLP, or any external system — only the SpanCollector trait and NoopCollector.
SHALL NOT depend on the tracing crate or opentelemetry crate — trace context is self-contained.
SHALL NOT use wall-clock time for ID generation — only for span duration measurement.
SHALL NOT require application code to manually propagate trace contexts.
verification: cargo clippy -p liminal -- -D warnings passes clean.
tracing/mod.rs contains only pub mod and pub use — no logic.
TraceContext is Copy + Clone + Debug + PartialEq + Eq.
Span finishing is a no-op when no SpanCollector is installed.
No external tracing or opentelemetry crate dependency.
